title: Grewia biloba var. parviflora
what_description: Grewia biloba var. parviflora is a plant taxon classified under the family Malvaceae. It has a shrub-like habit with an average height of 4-6 meters, and its stem is quadrangular with a diameter of 5-10 cm, bearing simple, alternate, and entire leaves, which are elliptical, 7-15 cm long, and 2-5 cm wide, with a petiole of 1-2 cm. The inflorescences are axillary and cymose, with a maximum diameter of 10-15 cm, consisting of 5-10 small flowers, and the calyx is 5-parted with a diameter of 2-3 mm, while the corolla is 5-lobed, white, and 1-2 cm in diameter. The fruit is a capsule, 4-6 cm long, and 1-2 cm wide, with 5-10 seeds per capsule. Grewia biloba var. parviflora is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Grewia biloba var. parviflora are diabetes, which has been managed through its ability to regulate blood sugar levels, and hypertension, a condition that Grewia biloba var. parviflora has been found to alleviate by reducing blood pressure. It also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, effectively reducing inflammation, and has been used to treat osteoarthritis by reducing joint pain and swelling. Furthermore, Grewia biloba var. parviflora has been proven to be effective in treating skin infections, asthma, digestive issues, rheumatism, gastritis, diarrhea, and fever. Additionally, it has been found to have anti-cancer properties, preventing the growth of cancer cells, and has been used to treat anemia, fungal infections, bacterial infections, wound healing, respiratory problems, and eczema, while also managing colitis and preventing hypoglycemia.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Grewia biloba var. parviflora are attributed to its antioxidant properties, which are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant exhibits antimicrobial activity, effectively inhibiting the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Grewia biloba var. parviflora are mediated by saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. The analgesic properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been demonstrated to relieve pain and discomfort. Additionally, the hepatoprotective properties of Grewia biloba var. parviflora are largely due to its ability to protect the liver from damage caused by oxidative stress and toxins.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Grewia biloba var. parviflora are flavonoids, tannins, phenolic acids, quercetin, and kaempferol. These constituents have been identified through phytochemical analysis and are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, while tannins exhibit astringent and antiseptic properties. Quercetin and kaempferol, two of the most abundant flavonoids in Grewia biloba var. parviflora, have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and anticancer activities. The phenolic acids present in the plant, such as chlorogenic acid, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, further contributing to the plant's therapeutic potential.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Grewia biloba var. parviflora are Grewia biloba infusion, Grewia biloba decoction, Grewia biloba tincture, Grewia biloba tea and Grewia biloba extract. Grewia biloba infusion is prepared by steeping the dried leaves and stems in hot water to extract the bioactive compounds. Grewia biloba decoction is a more intense preparation made by boiling the plant material in water to release its medicinal properties. Grewia biloba tincture is a concentrated liquid extract obtained through the process of maceration or percolation, typically used for internal consumption. Grewia biloba extract is a semi-solid or powder form obtained through various methods such as solvent extraction, freeze-drying or spray drying, often used in pharmaceutical and cosmetic applications.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Grewia biloba var. parviflora improperly are experience nausea due to gastrointestinal irritation, develop diarrhea as a result of increased gut motility, exhibit diarrhea caused by the plant's anthraquinone glycosides, experience allergic reactions due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that can trigger an immune response, and experience allergic reactions that can lead to symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. These side effects occur when the plant is not used in its recommended dosage or is ingested in excessive amounts. The anthraquinone glycosides in Grewia biloba var. parviflora can cause gastrointestinal upset, leading to nausea and diarrhea. The sesquiterpene lactones in the plant can trigger an immune response, resulting in allergic reactions. The improper use of Grewia biloba var. parviflora can also lead to other side effects, including liver toxicity and kidney damage, although these are less common.
